title: OKX, MetaMask, Matter Labs back dispute resolution court for AI agents
content: The Genlayer Foundation is leading the 27-firm consortium that makes AI-based payments, escrow and dispute resolution interoperable.
summary: The Genlayer Foundation, supported by OKX, MetaMask, and Matter Labs, is leading a consortium of 27 firms to create a dispute resolution court for AI agents. This initiative aims to make AI-based payments, escrow, and dispute resolution systems interoperable across various platforms.

title: A16z’s Andreessen lands Federal Reserve role as AI reshapes policy debate
content: The Fed named a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen to co-lead an AI productivity and jobs task force under Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy review.
summary: Marc Andreessen, co-founder of a16z, has been appointed to co-lead a Federal Reserve task force focused on AI productivity and jobs. This move highlights the increasing significance of artificial intelligence in high-level economic policy discussions.

title: Will the crypto lobby's $189M campaign get CLARITY over the line?
content: As the CLARITY Act advances, crypto's growing political influence in Washington is under scrutiny. But the crypto lobby's massive campaign spend is only part of the story.
summary: The CLARITY Act's progression is drawing attention to the crypto industry's increasing political influence and substantial lobbying expenditures in Washington D.C. The article examines the impact of this extensive campaign on potential legislative outcomes.

title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
content: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.
summary: The Bitcoin BIP 110 proposal, aimed at capping arbitrary data for a year, is nearing its deadline with no miner support. Key figures like Michael Saylor and Adam Back warn that turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create greater risks for the network.

title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
content: Bonzo Lend lost approximately $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract on the Hedera network.
summary: The lending protocol Bonzo Lend suffered a substantial loss of approximately $9.05 million due to an exploit on the Hedera network. An attacker leveraged a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract, causing Bonzo Lend's total value locked to plummet by 77%.

title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
content: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.
summary: The crypto IPO market is experiencing a stall due to funding constraints and investor caution, with capital instead rotating to AI and macro uncertainty. Christian Lopez of Cohen & Company Capital Markets attributes the delay to these factors rather than regulation. This shift in capital highlights a notable trend in investor interest.

title: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
content: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.
summary: According to Wirex CEO Chet Shah, the UK is finally demonstrating a serious approach to cryptocurrency through several recent regulatory steps. This shift indicates that the UK government may be moving past its previous indecisiveness regarding crypto policy.

title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
content: It's a sign of the times as the troubled company swaps its bitcoin treasury ambitions for AI data centers.
summary: Empery Digital, a company focused on Bitcoin treasury, has sold roughly half of its BTC holdings. This strategic shift indicates the troubled company is pivoting its ambitions from cryptocurrency to investing in AI data centers.

title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
content: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.
summary: The Ethereum Foundation deployed coordinated AI agents to scan its validator software, leading to the discovery of a remotely triggerable crash bug. While the AI successfully identified this critical vulnerability, human intervention was necessary to confirm its authenticity, as the AI agents also generated a significant number of false positive findings. This highlights both the promise and current limitations of AI in blockchain security auditing.

title: Meta's Chief Data Officer Says Agentic Commerce is the "Next Tier of Business"
content: Alex Schultz says stablecoins are assumed inside Meta. The harder problem is getting the rest of the world there.
summary: Meta's Chief Data Officer, Alex Schultz, identifies "Agentic Commerce" as the next major phase of business, highlighting its importance for the company's future. He notes that while stablecoins are an accepted norm within Meta, the primary challenge lies in achieving their widespread global adoption.

title: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
content: Despite President Donald Trump's refusal to sign Congress' bipartisan housing bill, it's set to go into effect at midnight, and its temporary CBDC ban along with it.
summary: A temporary ban on a U.S. government digital dollar (CBDC) is set to go into effect tonight as part of a bipartisan housing bill. The legislation will become law at midnight despite President Trump's refusal to sign it, implementing a limit on central bank digital currency.

title: Hyundai becomes first major South Korean company to introduce internal stablecoin transfers
content: The initiative builds on a broader shift by companies exploring stablecoins to move money between international operations more efficiently.
summary: Hyundai has become the first major South Korean company to implement internal stablecoin transfers. This initiative aligns with a growing trend of companies utilizing stablecoins to improve the efficiency of international money movement between their operations.

title: Circle soars after securing U.S. trust bank approval in crypto expansion
content: The approval adds to a growing list of crypto firms seeking federal banking licenses as the industry moves into the regulated financial system.
summary: Circle, a prominent crypto firm, has successfully secured U.S. trust bank approval, marking a significant step in its expansion within the regulated financial system. This approval is part of a growing trend where numerous cryptocurrency companies are seeking federal banking licenses, indicating the industry's movement towards integration with traditional finance.

title: Bitcoin zips higher to nearly $64,000 as chip rally and yen strength drive gains
content: Bitcoin is up 4.2% over seven days that contained an oil shock, a bond selloff and two rounds of U.S. strikes on Iran.
summary: Bitcoin's price has increased to nearly $64,000, with a 4.2% gain over the past seven days, despite significant global events such as an oil shock and US strikes on Iran. The Rally was driven by a chip rally and yen strength. This increase is notable given the current global economic climate.

title: Empery Digital shares rise after selling Bitcoin to fund AI data center project
content: The sales come months after a major Empery shareholder demanded the firm ditch its Bitcoin treasury strategy and force the resignation of its CEO and board.
summary: Empery Digital's shares increased after the company sold its Bitcoin treasury, a move intended to fund an AI data center project. This strategic pivot follows a major shareholder's demand for the firm to abandon its Bitcoin strategy.

title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
content: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.
summary: Bonzo Lend suffered a $9 million loss due to an oracle exploit on the Hedera network. An attacker exploited a flaw in Supra's on-chain oracle verifier to inflate the value of SAUCE collateral and borrow funds.

title: Dollar stablecoins could improve FX access but amplify currency runs: IMF paper
content: An IMF working paper says dollar stablecoins can improve access to foreign currency but may also help coordinate exits from local currencies during periods of severe exchange-rate stress.
summary: An IMF working paper suggests that dollar stablecoins could facilitate access to foreign currency, particularly in emerging markets. However, the paper also warns that these stablecoins might exacerbate currency runs by coordinating exits from local currencies during periods of significant exchange-rate instability.

title: Robinhood says its AI agent feature will ‘soon’ be assisting crypto traders
content: More than 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by Robinhood equities and options traders since late May, when Robinhood launched a beta version of the product.
summary: Robinhood announced that its AI agent feature will soon be available to assist crypto traders, building on its successful beta launch in late May. Over 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by equities and options traders since the product's initial release.

title: Kraken to overhaul app with AI investing assistant
content: The redesigned app will use AI to recommend trades and tailor investing tools around users' financial goals as Kraken expands into broader financial services.
summary: Kraken is redesigning its app to include an AI investing assistant that will provide users with personalized trade recommendations and tailored investing tools based on their financial goals. This overhaul is part of Kraken's expansion into broader financial services. The new app aims to enhance user experience and investment decisions.

title: Senate Democrats call for hearings into Trump’s ties to crypto amid CLARITY Act discussions
content: Five senators called for committee hearings to investigate US President Donald Trump’s policies potentially being influenced by crypto funding from United Arab Emirates-linked and other entities.
summary: Senate Democrats are calling for hearings to investigate if President Trump's policies are being influenced by crypto funding from entities linked to the United Arab Emirates. This comes amid discussions of the CLARITY Act. The investigation aims to shed light on potential ties between Trump and crypto donors.

title: US CBDC ban to go into effect without Trump signoff on housing bill
content: Donald Trump confirmed on social media that he would not sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a ban on a US CBDC until the end of 2030, setting the stage for the bill to become law on Saturday.
summary: The US is set to ban a central bank digital currency (CBDC) until 2030 as President Trump refuses to sign a housing bill containing the ban, allowing it to become law. The ban is expected to go into effect, shaping the country's stance on digital currency. This decision may impact the development of digital currency in the US.

title: Backpack joins race for 24/7 stock markets with tokenized equities
content: The launch comes as tokenized stocks become one of the fastest-growing corners of crypto, with exchanges and traditional finance firms racing to expand 24/7 trading.
summary: Backpack has entered the competition to provide 24/7 stock markets using tokenized equities. This move comes as tokenized stocks are rapidly growing within the crypto space, leading both traditional finance firms and exchanges to expand their 24/7 trading offerings.

title: Bitcoin whales sent BTC price to $64K as Coinbase Premium broke key level: CryptoQuant
content: Bitcoin price gains came thanks to US whales propeling the Coinbase Premium above a key trend line, new analysis from CryptoQuant reported.
summary: Bitcoin's price surged to $64,000, a move attributed to US whales pushing the Coinbase Premium above a crucial trend line. This analysis from CryptoQuant highlights the significant impact of large American investors on the BTC market.

title: MiCA licensing only the beginning as crypto custodians face scrutiny
content: A MiCA license allows crypto firms to operate in the EU, but the ESMA’s review will test whether custodians can meet the required security and resilience standards.
summary: While a MiCA license permits crypto firms to operate in the EU, it's merely the initial step for market entry. The ESMA will conduct further reviews to test whether these crypto custodians meet stringent security and resilience standards, indicating ongoing regulatory scrutiny beyond initial licensing.

title: USDC issuer Circle wins final approval for US national trust bank charter
content: Circle announces it received final OCC approval for a national trust bank that will initially serve the company and affiliates, with possible future custody services for institutional clients.
summary: Circle, the issuer of USDC, has received final approval for a national trust bank charter from the OCC, which will initially serve the company and its affiliates, with potential future expansion to custody services for institutional clients. This move marks a significant milestone for Circle and the broader crypto industry. The new bank is expected to strengthen Circle's financial capabilities and enhance its regulatory compliance.

title: Japanese lender launches Bitcoin-backed loans of up to $6.2M
content: CRYL is offering Bitcoin-backed loans to individuals and businesses as Japanese firms explore broader uses for BTC in lending and credit markets.
summary: A Japanese lender, CRYL, is offering Bitcoin-backed loans of up to $6.2M to individuals and businesses, exploring broader uses for BTC in lending and credit markets. This move signifies the growing adoption of cryptocurrency in traditional financial systems. The loans will help establish Bitcoin as a viable collateral option.

title: TeraWulf eyes $3.5B debt raise for Anthropic-linked data center: Report
content: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf is reportedly seeking $3.5 billion in debt financing led by Morgan Stanley for its Kentucky data center campus leased by AI company Anthropic.
summary: TeraWulf, a Bitcoin miner, is seeking $3.5 billion in debt financing for its Kentucky data center campus, which is leased by AI company Anthropic. The financing is reportedly being led by Morgan Stanley. This development highlights the growing connection between cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence companies.

title: Bitcoin returns to $64.3K with new 3-week BTC price highs imminent
content: Bitcoin bulls faced "crucial resistance" at $65,000 next as BTC price action diverged from oil and US dollar strength.
summary: Bitcoin's price has reached $64.3K and is expected to reach new three-week highs, with bulls facing resistance at $65,000. The BTC price movement is diverging from oil and US dollar strength. This development may indicate a potential surge in Bitcoin's value.

title: EU parliament passes ‘chat control,’ allowing private chat scans until 2028
content: EU lawmakers have again allowed tech firms to scan chats for abuse material, but exempted end-to-end encrypted messages.
summary: The EU parliament has passed a law allowing tech firms to scan private chats for abuse material until 2028, while exempting end-to-end encrypted messages. This decision aims to balance online safety with user privacy. The law's implications may impact tech companies and users alike.

title: Robinhood Chain sees over $70M in ETH bridged during first week
content: Robinhood’s choice to use Ethereum further solidifies its position as the “ultimate settlement layer and liquidity foundation for tokenized assets,” said HashKey Group’s Tim Sun.
summary: Robinhood Chain saw over $70M in ETH bridged during its first week, solidifying Ethereum's position as a settlement layer and liquidity foundation. This development is significant for the crypto community. Robinhood's choice to use Ethereum reflects the blockchain's growing importance.

title: Zcash targets July 28 launch for Ironwood network upgrade
content: Zcash’s next upgrade will replace the once-compromised Orchard pool and may offer clues about whether counterfeit tokens were created via a recent bug.
summary: Zcash is planning to launch its Ironwood network upgrade on July 28, replacing the compromised Orchard pool. The upgrade may provide insight into whether counterfeit tokens were created due to a recent bug. This upgrade aims to improve the security of the Zcash network.

title: Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend hit for $9 million after Supra verifier accepts manipulated price update
content: A second wallet borrowed another roughly $1 million but identified itself as a white hat hacker and said the funds would be returned.
summary: The Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend was exploited for $9 million due to a manipulated price update accepted by a Supra verifier. A second wallet borrowed $1 million but claimed to be a white hat hacker and promised to return the funds. The incident highlights a security vulnerability in the protocol.

title: Housing bill that includes a CBDC ban passed into law without Trump’s signature
content: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a provision that bans the Fed from issuing a CBDC, has become law.
summary: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which contains a provision banning the Federal Reserve from issuing a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), has officially become law. This legislation was enacted without former President Trump's signature, marking a significant development regarding digital currency policy in the United States.

title: DOJ to drop charges against man accused in $722 million BitClub crypto Ponzi scheme: Bloomberg
content: A former contestant on 'The Apprentice' is one of the persons urging the DOJ to drop the charges, the report said.
summary: The US Department of Justice is dropping charges against a man accused of involvement in a $722 million cryptocurrency Ponzi scheme called BitClub. A former contestant on 'The Apprentice' reportedly urged the DOJ to drop the charges. The scheme's total value is significant, indicating a major case in the crypto space.

title: Here’s what’s next for the Clarity Act as Congress returns to Washington
content: Congress returns to Washington next week, ready to take up cryptocurrency legislation before the midterm election calendar takes over.
summary: Congress is returning to Washington and will take up cryptocurrency legislation before the midterm elections. The legislation is expected to address various aspects of cryptocurrency. The return of Congress may bring significant changes to the crypto space.

title: SK Hynix’s $26.5 billion US listing brought to Telegram users via xStocks
content: Solana said that tokenized SK Hynix shares on SOL can be acquired through Backpack, xStocks and Ondo Finance.
summary: Tokenized shares of SK Hynix, linked to a $26.5 billion US listing, are now accessible on the Solana blockchain. These digital assets can be acquired by users through platforms such as Backpack, xStocks, and Ondo Finance.

title: Empery Digital offloads 1,400 bitcoins to help fund AI pivot, pay debt
content: Empery Digital used part of the proceeds for a 25% stake in an AI data center campus project.
summary: Empery Digital has sold 1,400 bitcoins to fund its pivot into AI and pay off debt, with some of the proceeds used to acquire a 25% stake in an AI data center campus project. This move marks a significant shift in the company's focus. The sale will help Empery Digital to restructure and invest in new technologies.

title: Kraken to relaunch its mobile app with agentic trading and advice at the center
content: Kraken is adding autonomous AI agents capable of monitoring markets and suggesting trades for users.
summary: Kraken is relaunching its mobile app with a focus on agentic trading and advice, utilizing autonomous AI agents to monitor markets and execute trades on behalf of users. The app will provide users with suggestions and automated trading capabilities. This update aims to enhance the user experience and provide more intuitive trading options.

title: Cambridge research puts 31% of Ethereum node activity in the US, where a third offline can stall finalization
content: Cambridge finds 31% of Ethereum node activity in the U.S., clustered on AWS, Hetzner and OVH, raising jurisdictional/counterparty questions.
summary: A Cambridge research study has found that 31% of Ethereum node activity is located in the US, with many nodes clustered on cloud services like AWS, Hetzner, and OVH. This raises concerns about jurisdictional and counterparty risks, as a significant portion of nodes being offline can stall finalization. The study highlights the potential vulnerabilities of the Ethereum network.

title: Binance co-CEO says 70% of EU withdrawals went to self-custody after MiCA deadline, with just 30% going to licensed platforms
content: Richard Teng said 70% of EU user withdrawals following its service suspension moved to self-custody rather than MiCA-regulated platforms.
summary: Binance's co-CEO, Richard Teng, reported that 70% of EU user withdrawals after the MiCA deadline opted for self-custody wallets instead of regulated platforms. This highlights a significant preference among users for decentralized control in response to new European cryptocurrency regulations.

title: ‘Communication challenge, nothing more’: Standard Chartered keeps $100,000 bitcoin target, calls Strategy selloff a signaling problem
content: Standard Chartered retained its $100,000 end-2026 bitcoin target, calling Strategy's STRC pivot a signaling problem, not a solvency one.
summary: Standard Chartered has maintained its target of bitcoin reaching $100,000 by the end of 2026, despite recent market fluctuations. The company believes Strategy's pivot is a signaling problem, not a solvency issue. This suggests that Standard Chartered remains optimistic about bitcoin's long-term potential.

title: Stablecoin firm Circle wins final OCC approval to open national trust bank
content: Circle wins final OCC approval for First National Digital Currency Bank, with USDC reserve management planned for a later phase.
summary: Circle, a stablecoin firm, has received final approval from the OCC to open a national trust bank, with plans to manage USDC reserves in a later phase. This approval marks a significant milestone for the company and the crypto industry. The bank will be called First National Digital Currency Bank.

title: Ledger researchers disclose Tangem card flaw; Tangem says risk to everyday users is ‘virtually non-existent’
content: Ledger researchers disclosed a laser attack that resets Tangem card passwords by bypassing a recovery-state check in the firmware.
summary: Ledger researchers discovered a flaw in Tangem cards that allows attackers to reset passwords using a laser attack, bypassing security checks. Tangem claims the risk to everyday users is virtually non-existent. The vulnerability was found in the firmware's recovery-state check.

title: Revolut integrates its crypto exchange with AI assistants as agentic trading spreads
content: Revolut X now connects to Claude, Gemini, OpenClaw, and Cursor, letting crypto users analyze, backtest, and execute trades via AI prompts.
summary: Revolut X has integrated its crypto exchange with AI assistants, including Claude, Gemini, OpenClaw, and Cursor, allowing users to analyze, backtest, and execute trades using AI prompts. This integration enables users to leverage AI capabilities for more informed trading decisions. The move marks a significant step in the adoption of AI in cryptocurrency trading.

title: Polymarket files applications to offer regulated margin trading in US
content: Polymarket's rival platform, Kalshi, already received US regulatory approval to provide margin trading back in March.
summary: Polymarket has filed applications to offer regulated margin trading in the US, following in the footsteps of its rival Kalshi, which received approval in March. This move aims to provide a regulated platform for users to engage in margin trading. The development is significant for the crypto community.

title: U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
Rep. French Hill urged the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act before the August recess, arguing the bill would establish a transparent crypto market framework that addresses ethics concerns surrounding President Trump's digital asset ventures.
This post U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: US Representatives are urging the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act in July, which aims to establish a transparent crypto market framework and address ethics concerns. The bill is seen as a way to provide clarity on digital asset regulation. Rep. French Hill argues that passing the bill before the August recess is crucial.

title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.
This post Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Circle has received final approval from the OCC to establish a national trust bank, enabling it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expand its digital asset custody business. This move allows for greater regulatory oversight and stability. Circle's national trust bank will operate under federal supervision.

title: Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
Metaplanet is taking its Bitcoin strategy beyond treasury accumulation, launching a study to explore tokenized, Bitcoin-backed credit products that could reshape Japan's debt market.
This post Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Metaplanet is launching a joint study to introduce Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan, aiming to transform the country's debt market. This initiative goes beyond Metaplanet's existing Bitcoin accumulation strategy. The study will explore tokenized credit products backed by Bitcoin.

title: Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
Standard Chartered reaffirmed its $100,000 end-2026 Bitcoin price target, calling Bitcoin "a screaming buy" and arguing that Strategy's recent sell-off reflects a communication issue around its new monetization strategy rather than a weakening Bitcoin outlook.
This post Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Standard Chartered has reaffirmed its $100,000 Bitcoin price target by the end of 2026, calling it a 'screaming buy'. The company attributes Strategy's recent sell-off to a communication issue, not a weakening Bitcoin outlook. This suggests a positive outlook for Bitcoin's future value.

title: JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
JPMorgan says Strategy's Bitcoin sales are a short-term concern, but the bigger long-term risk is that banks and institutions adopt private blockchains over public networks, reducing activity and capital flows across the broader crypto ecosystem.
This post JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: JPMorgan believes the real long-term threat to Bitcoin is not Strategy's Bitcoin sales, but rather the adoption of private blockchains by banks and institutions, which could reduce activity and capital flows in the crypto ecosystem. This could have a significant impact on the broader crypto market. The institution views this as a bigger risk than short-term sales.

title: Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
BitcoinTreasuries.net says June marked the first major stress test for Bitcoin-backed preferred shares, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the growing corporate Bitcoin financing model.
This post Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin-backed preferred shares faced their first major stress test in June, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the corporate Bitcoin financing model. This test is significant for the growing Bitcoin financing sector. The outcome has reinforced confidence in the model.

title: New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
New Hampshire's Executive Council voted 3-2 to reject a proposed $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, shelving what supporters said would have been the world's first such issuance despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers.
This post New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: New Hampshire's Executive Council rejected a $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, which would have been the world's first such issuance. The proposal was voted down 3-2, despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers. The rejection shelves a potentially groundbreaking financial instrument.

title: BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
BitGo launched new quantum-risk tools that help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets.
This post BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: BitGo has launched new tools to help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets. This move aims to mitigate the risks associated with quantum computing for Bitcoin custody. The tools will help holders protect their assets from potential quantum-related threats.

title: Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading
Russia's largest private lender, Alfa-Bank, plans to launch Bitcoin and crypto trading, custody, and a digital depository once new regulations take effect, joining a broader push by major Russian banks to enter the country's emerging crypto market by late 2026 or 2027.
This post Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Alfa-Bank, Russia's largest private lender, plans to launch Bitcoin and crypto trading, custody, and a digital depository once new regulations take effect. This move is part of a broader push by major Russian banks to enter the country's emerging crypto market. The launch is expected to occur by late 2026 or 2027.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they…
summary: Anthropic has gained insight into the inner workings of large language models, including Claude, by finding a hidden space where they process concepts. This discovery sheds light on how these models think and understand information. The finding is significant for the development of AI technology.

title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
content: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…
summary: Anthropic developed a technique to better understand large language models, finding a range of results from mundane to unnerving. The tool, called the Jacobian lens, provides insight into the models' thought processes. This discovery could significantly impact AI development.

title: EmTech AI 2026: The Rise of the AI Platform
summary: The EmTech AI 2026 conference discusses the growing importance of AI platforms, highlighting their potential to transform industries and revolutionize the way we live and work. The conference explores the latest advancements in AI technology and its applications. AI platforms are expected to play a crucial role in shaping the future of various sectors.

title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI is back in the spotlight, with reports that he is discussing giving…
summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman proposes that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI, with a potential $300 stake for families. This idea is being discussed, sparking interest in the tech community. The Treasury also issued an AI warning, highlighting the significance of AI's impact.

title: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
content: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six months into the future. Returning to the foundational elements of AI architecture—the…
summary: The article discusses the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the importance of understanding the foundational elements of AI architecture for IT leaders to scale and make valuable investments. It highlights the evolution of AI and the introduction of risk, emphasizing the need for a solid foundation. The article aims to guide IT leaders in navigating the growing technology.

title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
content: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that the company stole trade secrets, with senior leadership, including a former Apple employee, involved in the misconduct. The lawsuit claims that OpenAI's actions were intentional and directed by its top officials. This case has significant implications for the tech industry.

title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue states that open source AI is booming, with the company becoming a hub for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, now used by half of the Fortune 500. This growth indicates a significant shift in the AI industry. Hugging Face has become a key player in the open source AI space.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
content: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.
summary: SK Hynix has raised $26.5B in the largest foreign IPO in US history, driven by the AI chip boom. The company, along with Samsung, is being urged to build new factories in the US. This development highlights the growing importance of AI chips and the need for domestic manufacturing.

title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face's CEO, Clem Delangue, states that open source AI is booming, with the company serving as a platform for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, used by half of the Fortune 500. Companies are shifting away from renting AI and towards open source solutions. This trend indicates a significant change in the AI industry.

title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.
summary: OpenAI's new models, including GPT 5.6, will continue to power Microsoft's workplace and productivity apps, amid speculation of a potential breakup between the two companies. This ensures continuity for Microsoft's Copilot 365. The partnership remains intact for now.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
content: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 executive, is stepping down from her role after a longer-than-expected medical leave, creating a leadership vacuum at a crucial time for the company. OpenAI is considering an IPO and competing with Anthropic in the enterprise market. This change may impact OpenAI's strategic direction and growth plans.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
content: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.
summary: OpenAI has launched a new family of models, GPT-5.6, which promises improvements in various areas, including cybersecurity. The new models are expected to enhance performance and capabilities. This launch is a significant update from OpenAI.

title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
content: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.
summary: Lyzr, a startup building AI agents, used its own AI agent to raise $100 million, demonstrating the product's effectiveness. This achievement showcases the potential of AI in handling complex tasks, such as fundraising. The successful raise highlights the viability of AI agents in enterprise settings.

title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
content: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.
summary: OpenAI is shutting down its AI-powered browser Atlas after less than a year, but will integrate some features into its desktop app and a Chrome extension. This move indicates a shift in OpenAI's browser ambitions. The company is re strategizing its approach to AI-powered browsing.

title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
content: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.
summary: Elon Musk has expressed support for Mythos/Fable and promised not to limit Anthropic's access to its models, which could impact the company's $40 billion revenue. This statement aims to reassure Anthropic amidst potential concerns. Musk's assurance is significant for Anthropic's future

title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
content: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.
summary: The AI ROI debate has resurfaced with significantly larger numbers and potential consequences, prompting a discussion on the value of AI with a $3 trillion question. This debate may have substantial implications for the future of AI development and investment. The question is whether AI can provide a worthwhile return on investment.

title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
content: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.
summary: Meta has launched Muse Spark 1.1, an AI coding tool that can handle large workloads, fix bugs, and assist with code migrations, targeting enterprise clients. This move marks Meta's entry into the competitive AI coding market. The tool aims to provide automation solutions that enterprises increasingly need.

title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
content: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
summary: OpenAI is accused of hiding evidence in a copyright trial related to ChatGPT, with news publishers claiming the company concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The lawsuit has been escalated with a new motion for sanctions. This development may have significant implications for AI copyright laws.

title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
content: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.
summary: Google will now require advertisers to disclose when their ads use artificial intelligence to create synthetic or digitally altered content, extending a policy previously only applied to election ads. This change aims to increase transparency in online advertising. The move is part of Google's efforts to prevent misleading ads.

title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
content: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."
summary: Gradium, a Paris-based AI voice startup, has raised $100M in seed funding backed by Nvidia. The company plans to use the funds to open a new office in the Bay Area and compete for top talent. This move aims to strengthen its position in the global AI ecosystem.

title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
content: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."
summary: The government's decision to release OpenAI's frontier model is unclear, with details of the dialogue between the government and OpenAI and Anthropic not being disclosed. The decision-making process behind this release is still shrouded in mystery. This has raised questions about the safety and implications of releasing such models.

title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
content: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.
summary: Meta will begin production of its new AI-specific chips in September, aiming to reduce reliance on GPU providers like Nvidia. This move is part of Meta's efforts to optimize its AI infrastructure. The new chips are designed to improve AI processing efficiency.

title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
content:
Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission.
"Earlier this week, we announced that one way for people to generate images in Meta AI is by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they want to reference," Meta says in an update to a blog post about its new Muse Image AI model. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give pe …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Meta is shutting down an Instagram feature that allowed users to create AI deepfakes of public accounts without permission, following significant backlash. The feature was intended to be a creative tool but raised concerns about user consent. It has been discontinued just days after its announcement.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
content:
Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, which OpenAI bought in 2025 - along with two specific employees, Tang Tan (OpenAI's chief hardware officer) and Chang Liu (who joined OpenAI from Apple in January).
An Apple spokesperson shared this statement with 9to5Mac:
At Apple, our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the b …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to hardware, claiming that former Apple employees now at OpenAI used this information to advance the startup's hardware plans. The lawsuit also names IO Products and two specific employees. This lawsuit could have significant implications for both Apple and OpenAI.

title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
content:
Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let you know if content is AI content or not."
At the same time, Mosseri seems to be drawing a distinction between content-based sorting and banning AI from the platform entirely. In fact, he believes people who love AI content "should be able to have a feed that's just AI town." Instagram, like many other platforms, including TikTok, YouTube, and Fac …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri stated that users who don't like AI-generated content shouldn't have it in their feed, and instead, the platform should let users know if content is AI-generated or not. Mosseri believes in giving users the option to have an AI-only feed if they prefer. He draws a distinction between sorting and banning AI content entirely.

title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
content:
A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes equipped with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems." Customers will be "compensated" for participating in the pilot program. 
Sunrun plans to sell the distributed compute power from the nodes to "enterprise compute buyers," like AI companies. It's a new approach to finding resources and space for AI compute infrastructure, as da …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Sunrun, a solar and home energy storage company, is launching a pilot program to place AI compute units in customers' homes, compensating them for participation, and selling the distributed compute power to enterprise buyers. The program aims to provide a new approach to AI compute infrastructure. Customers with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems are eligible to participate.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
content:
OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X.
The news follows Simo's original announcement in April that she would take a few weeks of medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition, shortly after she had taken on the AGI chief title. (She had formerly been the company's CEO of applications). Around the same time, COO Brad Lightcap also stepped down from his role to focus on "special projects," and OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch also stepped down in order to focus on her health. Rouch planned to return to a "more narrowly scoped role" when her health  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's AGI chief, is stepping down due to a neuroimmune condition and will transition to a part-time advisor role. She had taken on the AGI chief title after previously serving as CEO of applications. The move follows other recent departures from OpenAI, including COO Brad Lightcap and CMO Kate Rouch.

title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
content:
You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can find it by tapping the three dots or info button on ads, which pulls up the same panel where you can also block or report ads.
Google says it will automatically apply the AI label to any ads made with its own generative AI advertising tools, but AI ads made elsewhere will need to have the label applied manually. In some regions, Google's new AI  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google has introduced a new feature that labels ads created or edited with AI, visible in the 'My Ad Center' section on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube. The label will be automatically applied to ads made with Google's generative AI tools, while others will require manual labeling. This update aims to increase transparency in ad creation.

title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
content:
Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of security updates included in each security release." 
Hackers, even amateurs, have increasingly been using AI to quickly exploit security weaknesses over the past several months. Security researchers are also using AI to find issues faster, leading to more frequent high-severity vulnerabilities, like the "Copy Fail" exploit that impacted nearly every Linux distribution in May. Similarly, when Anthropic announce …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft is using AI to identify potential security issues earlier, resulting in a higher volume of security updates in each release, as hackers and security researchers increasingly use AI to exploit and find vulnerabilities. This is in response to the growing use of AI by hackers to quickly exploit security weaknesses. The move aims to improve Windows 11 security.

title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
content:
About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the Trump administration's greenlight for a public rollout of the model. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called it "the best model we have ever produced."
To celebrate, OpenAI also unveiled a new AI agent on the same day: ChatGPT Work. It's billed as a combination of ChatGPT and Codex, allowing the everyday non-technical user to take advantage of Codex's capabilities for non-coding tasks, and it's powered by the GPT-5.6 model suite (Sol, Terra, and Luna). "It can g …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI has rolled out its new GPT-5.6 model after receiving government approval, with CEO Sam Altman calling it the company's best model yet. The rollout was previously limited to government-approved organizations. OpenAI also unveiled ChatGPT Work, a new AI agent that combines ChatGPT and Codex.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
content: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."
summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
content: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.
summary: A preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of using humanoid robots controlled by surgeons to perform operations, with a world-first operation being conducted on live pigs. This trial aims to assess the potential of humanoid robots in surgery. The use of robots in surgery could potentially revolutionize the field.

title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
content: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.
summary: OpenAI is facing potential sanctions for allegedly hiding and deleting ChatGPT logs in a copyright dispute with the New York Times. The case could have significant implications for AI companies and their handling of user data. OpenAI's actions may be seen as a misstep in its legal battle with news organizations.

title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
content: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
summary: An Ivy League professor suspected AI cheating and ordered an in-person final, which resulted in scores falling by 50%. The professor believes AI cheating can lead to a failed society. This highlights the growing concern of AI-assisted cheating in academic settings.

title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
content: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.
summary: A man allegedly used AI software Grok to create explicit images of his stepdaughter, and later took his own life. This incident is part of a larger lawsuit against the company behind Grok, accused of enabling child predators. Multiple young girls are suing the company for its handling of child sexual abuse material.

title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
content: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
summary: Hackers can utilize 9 popular AI tools to create massive botnets by exploiting the limitations of large language models, specifically their inability to respond with uncertainty. This vulnerability, known as HalluSquatting, allows attackers to assemble and control botnets. The discovery highlights potential security risks associated with AI tools.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development is made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence. The technology has the potential to significantly impact various industries and daily life.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
content: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.
summary: Anthropic, a company known for its AI technology, has been accused of spying on users through a secret tracker in its Claude tool, contradicting its anti-surveillance stance. An engineer stated that the experiment is now over. This incident raises concerns about user privacy and trust in AI companies.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
content: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.
summary: A UK regulator has warned of an 'arms race' to keep up with the increasing use of AI in financial services, with millions using the technology for personal finance decisions. The regulator is calling for greater powers to oversee the industry. This warning highlights the growing importance of AI in finance.

title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
content: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, permitting their global release. This follows earlier safety testing prompted by concerns raised by former President Trump.

title: Bitcoin analysts predict $300,000–$500,000 price in 2029. The math says no
content: Analysts predict a rally to $300,000 or more by 2029. But key data suggests the era of moonshots may be over.
summary: Bitcoin analysts are predicting a significant price rally to $300,000–$500,000 by 2029. However, the article argues that crucial data indicates the period of such extreme price increases for Bitcoin might be coming to an end.

title: Polymarket seeks approval to bring margin trading to U.S. customers
content: Polymarket's application to allow users to take positions that are not fully collateralized follows authorization granted to rival Kalshi in March.
summary: Polymarket is applying for U.S. regulatory approval to introduce margin trading for its customers, enabling them to take leveraged positions. This initiative follows a similar authorization granted to its competitor, Kalshi, in March.

title: Bitcoin's $60,000-$70,000 range becomes third most traded range in history
content: Bitcoin has spent 307 days in the $60,000-$70,000 range, the third longest consolidation in any $10,000 price band.
summary: Bitcoin has spent 307 days trading within the $60,000-$70,000 price range, making it the third longest consolidation period in any $10,000 price band. This signifies a significant period of stability or indecision at a high valuation for the cryptocurrency.

title: Metaplanet explores bringing bitcoin-backed digital credit to Japan
content: The bitcoin treasury company is working with JPYC and Progmat to explore tokenized credit products backed by bitcoin, aiming to create efficient, 24/7 credit markets in Japan.
summary: Metaplanet, a bitcoin treasury company, is collaborating with JPYC and Progmat to explore the development of bitcoin-backed tokenized credit products in Japan. This initiative aims to establish efficient, 24/7 digital credit markets utilizing bitcoin as collateral.

title: Live updates: Bitcoin rises to $64,000 as SK Hynix opens for trade after $26.5 billion IPO
content: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.
summary: On Thursday, spot Bitcoin funds experienced outflows of approximately $95 million, while ether funds saw roughly $52 million in outflows, ending their five-day inflow streak. These significant institutional outflows occurred despite a general rally in cryptocurrency prices.

title: New Hampshire snuffs out trailblazing state-government bitcoin bond effort
content: At its last stage for government approval, the state's executive council rejected the bond project 3-2.
summary: New Hampshire's executive council rejected a pioneering state-government bitcoin bond project in a 3-2 vote. This decision, made at its last stage for government approval, effectively snuffs out the effort to integrate cryptocurrency into state financial operations.

title: With SEC fight over, Coinbase's top legal exec Grewal moves on, and others reassigned
content: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal will depart the exchange, though he'll continue as an adviser, he said Thursday.
summary: Coinbase's Chief Legal Officer, Paul Grewal, is departing from his executive role at the exchange. He will, however, remain with the company as an advisor.

title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
content: The ongoing debate comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.
summary: Prominent Bitcoin figures Michael Saylor and Adam Back have criticized the BIP-110 Ordinals proposal. This debate persists even though Ordinals transaction activity has broadly declined over the last two years.

title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
content: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.
summary: Ethereum saw a 3% price increase, attributed to a surge in tokenization and institutional investment. However, weak on-chain and derivatives data indicate that ETH could still retest the $1,700 support level.

title: Bitcoin price gains nearly 10% in July, but traders still see BTC copying 2022 bear market
content: Bitcoin price gains approached 10% for the first two weeks of the month, but analysis warned of the bear market returning from August onward.
summary: Bitcoin's price increased by nearly 10% in the first two weeks of July. However, analysis warns that the cryptocurrency could re-enter a bear market from August onwards, mirroring trends seen in 2022.

title: DOJ moves to dismiss charges against alleged $722M BitClub fraudster: Report
content: Matthew Goettsche was set to face trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities.
summary: The Department of Justice is reportedly seeking to dismiss charges against Matthew Goettsche, a key figure in the alleged $722 million BitClub Ponzi scheme. Goettsche had been facing trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities related to the cryptocurrency operation.

title: Crypto Biz: How stablecoins found their niche
content: Stablecoins are carving out specialized roles as regulation reshapes the market, while Strategy’s Bitcoin sale and Vanguard’s tokenization push highlight crypto’s evolving financial landscape.
summary: Stablecoins are increasingly finding specialized roles within the cryptocurrency market as regulatory frameworks evolve. This shift, alongside Strategy's Bitcoin sale and Vanguard's exploration of tokenization, signifies a continually evolving financial landscape for digital assets.

title: Wall Street banks tighten prediction market rules for staff as insider fears spread
content: Wall Street banks, including Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, are restricting employee prediction market trades as insider trading fears spread across Polymarket and Kalshi.
summary: Wall Street banks, including Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, are tightening rules around employee trading on prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi. This decision stems from increasing fears of potential insider trading activities on these platforms.

title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
content: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.
summary: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to develop ChatGPT experiences specifically for families, caregivers, and older adults. This move indicates a strategic effort by the company to expand the AI's reach and integration into everyday household use.

title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
content: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.
summary: Insiders report that Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration about OpenAI offering a 5% stake in the United States, a figure that is much lower than the target suggested by Senator Sanders. The move highlights potential political involvement and investment discussions surrounding the AI firm.

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.
summary: Google's rapid expansion of AI workloads is projected to raise its electricity consumption by 37% in 2025. The company is attempting to offset the resulting emissions by increasing its use of clean energy sources in its data centers.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: Researchers have demonstrated a new jailbreak attack where simply convincing a large language model that 2 + 2 = 5 causes it to execute prohibited instructions, highlighting another drawback of AI-driven browsers.

title: Bitcoin gets a green light from a reliable momentum gauge. Here are key levels to watch
content: A smoother long-term MACD just flipped bullish, pointing to more upside. Here are key levels that will decide if the ongoing bounce turns into a full blown uptrend.
summary: A long-term MACD indicator for Bitcoin has just turned bullish, signaling potential for further price increases. The article highlights key levels that will determine if the current bounce develops into a sustained uptrend.

title: XRP up 2% as buyers push through $1.10 resistance
content: A late-session volume surge carried XRP above its recent range, with traders now watching whether $1.10 can hold as support.
summary: XRP's price has increased by 2% after buyers pushed through the $1.10 resistance level, with traders now monitoring if this level can hold as support. The late-session volume surge led to this price movement. The cryptocurrency's next steps are being closely watched by traders.

title: Bitcoin's quiet split: Strong in USD, lagging in JPY as Yen rises on intervention fears
content: A sharp rise in the yen has left bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies underperforming in yen terms compared with their dollar-based trading pairs.
summary: Bitcoin is performing strongly in USD but lagging in JPY due to a sharp rise in the yen. This disparity is caused by intervention fears, affecting Bitcoin's value in yen terms. The difference highlights the impact of currency fluctuations on cryptocurrency trading pairs.

title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
content: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.
summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts believes Bitcoin is nearing the late stages of its bear market and could reach $250,000 within the next couple of years. While he considers a $1 million target by 2030 premature, he expresses confidence in Bitcoin's significant near-term price appreciation.

title: New Hampshire council votes down $100M Bitcoin bonds
content: State representative Keith Ammon said the New Hampshire executive council's vote against Bitcoin-backed bonds was “short-sighted,” urging the body to reconsider the measure.
summary: The New Hampshire executive council has rejected a proposal for $100 million in Bitcoin-backed bonds. State representative Keith Ammon criticized the decision as "short-sighted" and called for the council to reconsider the measure.

title: Metaplanet explores Bitcoin-backed digital credit with JPYC in Japan
content: Metaplanet said it is studying Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan with JPYC and Progmat, but no product has been launched.
summary: Metaplanet is exploring Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan alongside JPYC and Progmat, although no product has been launched yet. The collaboration aims to introduce new financial offerings in the Japanese market. This development has the potential to bridge cryptocurrency and traditional finance.

title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
content: The preceding eight weeks drained a combined $9.46 billion from the two groups, meaning this week’s rebound recovered only about 3% of those outflows.
summary: Bitcoin and ether ETFs recorded a combined inflow of $282 million, breaking an eight-week streak of outflows. However, this inflow only recovered approximately 3% of the $9.46 billion that had exited the funds during the preceding period.

title: New Hampshire’s $100 million bitcoin bond proposal fails to pass final vote
content: Governor Ayotte had expressed support for the proposal, touting it as an innovative way to bring more investment opportunities to the state.
summary: A proposal for a $100 million bitcoin bond in New Hampshire has failed to pass its final vote, despite support from Governor Ayotte who saw it as a way to bring more investment to the state. The proposal's failure may impact the state's ability to attract crypto-related investments. The news may have implications for the crypto community.

title: DOJ charges federal inmate over alleged theft of $290,000 in crypto forfeited to US government
content: Federal inmate Rossen Iossifov faces new charges for allegedly taking $290,000 in government-forfeited cryptocurrency.
summary: A federal inmate, Rossen Iossifov, has been charged with allegedly stealing $290,000 in cryptocurrency that had been forfeited to the US government. The incident highlights a security concern in the management of seized digital assets. The charges are part of a broader effort to prevent the misuse of forfeited funds.

title: Cathie Wood’s Ark Invest buys $14 million worth of Circle, offloads Robinhood shares
content: Circle Internet Group's stock dropped 1.65% during Thursday's trading session, extending its one-month decline to 20.2%.
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest purchased $14 million worth of Circle Internet Group's stock, while selling its shares of Robinhood, amidst a decline in Circle's stock price. Circle's stock dropped 1.65% on Thursday, adding to its one-month decline of 20.2%. This move may indicate a strategic shift in Ark Invest's portfolio.

title: UK Labour MPs push to permanently ban crypto political donations
content: Labour MPs are gathering support for proposed amendments to a key bill that would permanently ban crypto political donations.
summary: UK Labour MPs are proposing amendments to ban crypto political donations permanently. The move aims to gather support for the changes to a key bill. This could impact the way political campaigns are funded in the UK.

title: North Carolina passes bill recognizing CFTC preemption over prediction markets
content: North Carolina's new law taxes prediction market platforms at 6% of their net trading fee revenue that is attributable to North Carolina residents.
summary: North Carolina has passed a bill that recognizes the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) preemption over prediction markets and imposes a 6% tax on prediction market platforms' net trading fee revenue from state residents. This law affects how prediction markets operate in the state. The move may have implications for the broader regulatory environment of prediction markets.

title: Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher
Bitcoin slipped to around $62,000 after rebounding from last week's $57,700 low, while CryptoQuant says improving demand, seasonality and valuation support further gains, though its bearish Bull Score Index suggests the move remains a bear-market recovery rather than a full trend reversal.
This post Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin's price slipped to $62,000 after a rebound from $57,700, with CryptoQuant suggesting potential for further gains due to improving demand and seasonality. However, the Bull Score Index indicates this may be a bear-market recovery rather than a trend reversal. The cryptocurrency's valuation is seen as supportive of higher prices.

title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
content: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."
summary: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram after receiving backlash, stating that the feature missed its intended mark of providing a useful creative tool. The feature allowed public content to be referenced in certain ways, but users responded negatively. The company has since made the feature unavailable.

title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
content: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.
summary: Instagram users can stop Meta's AI from using their photos by changing their account settings to private, as Meta's AI can use photos from public accounts to generate AI images. This is done through a feature called Muse Image, which allows users to create AI-generated images using public photos. Users can protect their photos by making their account private.

title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
content:
OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an August 9th date for deprecation.
The shutdown follows OpenAI's push to reduce "side quests" and catch up with Anthropic on productivity features. As part of that push, in March, The Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI planned to combine the ChatGPT app, Codex, and Atlas into a desktop "superapp" - ChatGPT Work appears to be the result …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI is shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, a browser that could perform tasks on behalf of users, less than a year after its launch. The decision is part of OpenAI's effort to reduce side projects and focus on productivity features. ChatGPT Atlas will be deprecated on August 9th.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
content: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.
summary: Google has unveiled the Nano Banana 2 Lite, an image generation model that is the fastest and cheapest in its series. It can create images in just a few seconds, though the visual quality is lower than more advanced models.

title: Crypto defies equity weakness as altcoin optimism builds into the weekend
content: The price of bitcoin hit $64,400, retesting a level it failed to penetrate on Monday, with a break higher opening the path toward the June 15 peak of $67,250.
