summary: Bitcoin's price surged to $64,400, retesting a key resistance level previously failed on Monday. A successful breakthrough could pave the way for Bitcoin to reach its June 15 peak of $67,250, signaling growing optimism for altcoins despite broader equity market weakness.

title: US charges prisoner over alleged laundering of seized Kraken crypto
content: US prosecutors charged Rossen Iossifov over the alleged laundering of $290,000 in forfeited crypto from a Kraken account.
summary: US prosecutors have charged Rossen Iossifov, an existing prisoner, with allegedly laundering $290,000 in cryptocurrency. The funds were reportedly forfeited crypto from a Kraken account.

title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
content: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.
summary: The National Design Studio has delayed its plan to overhaul .gov website standards originally proposed under Trump, citing challenges that have resulted in AI-generated design proposals that are considered problematic. The delay highlights concerns over the use of AI in public web design.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides a daily roundup of the latest news affecting the cryptocurrency market, covering topics such as Bitcoin price movements, blockchain developments, DeFi, Web3, and regulatory updates. It serves as a quick guide to current trends and events in the crypto space.

title: Japan's 'invest locally' plan likely to spur demand for assets like bitcoin, gold
content: Your day-ahead look for July 10, 2026
summary: The article title suggests a potential surge in demand for assets like Bitcoin and gold due to Japan's 'invest locally' plan. However, the provided content is merely a placeholder for a 'day-ahead look' scheduled for July 10, 2026, offering no further details on the policy or its implications.

title: Grayscale's CFO exits after 7 years with crypto asset manager
content: Edward McGee stepped down after seven years, months after distribution chief John Hoffman left for Ondo Finance.
summary: Edward McGee, the Chief Financial Officer of crypto asset manager Grayscale, has stepped down after seven years. This follows the earlier departure of distribution chief John Hoffman, who left for Ondo Finance months prior.

title: The Download: a nuclear landmark, and China eyes Nvidia chips
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US —Casey Crownhart I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year…
summary: Four nuclear reactors in the US have hit a milestone, and China is looking to acquire Nvidia chips, a significant development in the tech industry, although no direct connection to AI or crypto is mentioned in the given text

title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips to…
summary: A California dairy farmer is using worms and microbes to reduce manure pollution, a potential solution for environmental issues. This approach is part of a larger trend in using technology and nature to combat pollution. The method involves using worms to break down manure, reducing pollution and creating a more sustainable farming practice.

title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
content:
Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon emissions increased 25 percent in 2025, totalling 34 million metric tons "without select interventions." Microsoft says this was "driven primarily by the expansion of our datacenter infrastructure," as well as the company's decision last February to stop purchasing "non-additional, unbundled renewable energy certificates." 
Several years ago, Microsoft set itself a goal to be carbon negative by 2030, meaning it will need to remove more carbon emissions t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft's carbon emissions increased by 25% in 2025, driven by datacenter infrastructure expansion, according to its 2026 sustainability report. The company aims to be carbon negative by 2030. This increase may impact Microsoft's climate goals.

title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
content: Squeeze on Rust Belt electricity bills threatens Trump’s manufacturing plan.
summary: The energy demand of data centers is threatening Donald Trump's Made in America plan by squeezing Rust Belt electricity bills, which could impact manufacturing. This could hinder Trump's goal of revitalizing American industry. Data centers' high energy consumption is a significant contributor to this issue.

title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
content: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have identified those donors, he says. He…
summary: A European fertility group is calling for limits on sperm donors, citing concerns for individuals conceived through anonymous donation, like 47-year-old Ties van der Meer, who doesn't know how many siblings he has. The issue arose after a clinic in the Netherlands destroyed donor records. This affects people's ability to know their genetic lineage.

title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
content: I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power. Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can…
summary: Four US nuclear reactors have achieved a significant milestone, reaching criticality, a technical milestone showing a reactor can sustain a nuclear chain reaction. This achievement was set as a goal by the Trump administration last year. The reactors are microreactors, a newer type of nuclear reactor.

title: Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution
content: Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips on his family’s land in Hickman, a tiny town in the state’s agricultural heartland. He reaches down with both hands and pulls up a clump of muck, turning it over to reveal a half-dozen squirming red earthworms.…
summary: A California dairy farmer is using worms and microbes to address manure pollution on his land, a potential solution for the agricultural industry. The method involves using earthworms to break down manure in wood chips. This approach could help reduce pollution and create a more sustainable farming practice.
